title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true
input_row.description: Enable client authentication on etcd service.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should not be available to unauthenticated clients. You should enable the client authentication via valid certificates to secure the access to the etcd service.
input_row.audit: OpenShift uses X.509 certificates to provide secure communication to etcd. OpenShift installation generates these files and sets the arguments appropriately. The following certificates are generated and used by etcd and other processes that communicate with etcd: • Client certificates: Client certificates are currently used by the API server only, and no other service should connect to etcd directly except for the proxy. Client secrets ( etcd-client , etcd-metric-client , etcd-metric-signer , and etcd-signer ) are added to the openshift-config , openshift-monitoring , and openshift-kube-apiserver namespaces. • Server certificates: Used by the etcd server for authenticating client requests. Run the following command on the etcd server node: for i in $(oc get pods -oname -n openshift-etcd) do oc exec -n openshift-etcd -c etcd $i -- \ ps -o command= -C etcd | sed 's/.*\(--client-cert-auth=[^ ]*\).*/\1/' done Verify that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true for each etcd member.
input_row.remediation: This setting is managed by the cluster etcd operator. No remediation required. Internal Only - General
input_row.impact: All clients attempting to access the etcd server will require a valid client certificate.
input_row.default_value: By default, client-cert-auth is set to true .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the IBM Cloud Console**: Log into the IBM Cloud account and navigate to the OpenShift cluster.
2. **Obtain Access to the OpenShift Command Line**: Use the OpenShift CLI (`oc`) to connect to your cluster.
3. **Execute Command to Verify the --client-cert-auth Setting**: Run the following command to inspect the etcd pod settings:
   ```bash
   for i in $(oc get pods -oname -n openshift-etcd); do 
       oc exec -n openshift-etcd -c etcd $i -- ps -o command= -C etcd | sed 's/.*\(--client-cert-auth=[^ ]*\).*/\1/' 
   done
   ```
4. **What to Verify**: Check the output from the command above to see if the `--client-cert-auth` argument is set to `true` for each etcd member.
5. **Expected Secure Configuration**: The output should confirm that `--client-cert-auth=true` for all etcd instances.
6. **How to Confirm Compliance**: If all the etcd members are configured to use the client certificate authentication (indicated by `--client-cert-auth=true`), the control is compliant.

This manual approach involves direct interaction with the OpenShift cluster through the command line and relies on ensuring proper configuration of the etcd service to meet compliance requirements.
